name: CATIA
description: A high-end CAD platform used heavily in aircraft design, surfacing, configuration control, and integrated engineering workflows.
whyUseIt: CATIA is deeply embedded in major aerospace OEM environments because it handles complex geometry, assemblies, and controlled release workflows at industrial scale.
fieldPosition: Detailed design, release geometry, and cross-functional product definition
engineerWorkflow: Move from preliminary layout into controlled geometry, interfaces, drawings, and released design changes shared across multiple engineering teams.
managerExpects: Clean configuration control, disciplined release practice, and geometry that downstream analysis and manufacturing teams can trust.
demoDescription: Lay out a wing-box or systems-installation concept with enough fidelity for cross-team review and interface checking.
beginnerMistakes: Treating CAD completion as design completion without checking performance or manufacturability
Breaking configuration control with unmanaged local copies
